        hb_shape
      • hb_shape(font: HbFont; buf: HbBuffer; features: pointer; numFeatures: cuint)
        isHarfBuzzAvailable
      • isHarfBuzzAvailable(): bool
        loadFontFromFile
      • loadFontFromFile(path: string): HbFont
        shapeUtf8
      • shapeUtf8(font: HbFont; text: string; fontSize: float32): seq[ShapedGlyph]

Minimal Nim bindings to libharfbuzz. Covers the slice we need to shape a UTF-8 string with a TTF font and recover the glyph sequence with proper kerning, ligatures, and contextual forms.

This is not a full HarfBuzz binding; it exposes only the entry points the glyph atlas calls. See HarfBuzz's hb.h for the full API.

All procs use the harfbuzz dynlib, resolved via the standard library search path. macOS users get it from /opt/homebrew/lib/libharfbuzz.dylib; project-wide rpath (configured in config.nims) makes that work without DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH. On Linux, install via the system package manager (libharfbuzz-dev on Debian/Ubuntu, harfbuzz-devel on RHEL/Fedora).

Types

HarfBuzzAvailable = object
  available*: bool
  checked*: bool
Sentinel for whether HarfBuzz is loadable at runtime. Cached after the first successful load. 
HbBlob = distinct pointer
HbBuffer = distinct pointer
HbDirection {.size: 4.} = enum
  HB_DIRECTION_INVALID = 0, HB_DIRECTION_LTR = 4, HB_DIRECTION_RTL = 5,
  HB_DIRECTION_TTB = 6, HB_DIRECTION_BTT = 7
HbFace = distinct pointer
HbFeature {.bycopy.} = object
  tag*: uint32
  value*: uint32
  start*: cuint
HbFont = distinct pointer
HbGlyphInfo {.bycopy.} = object
  codepoint*: uint32         ## glyph index (post-shaping) or codepoint (pre)
  mask*: uint32
  cluster*: uint32           ## byte offset in source string
  var1*: uint32
  var2*: uint32
HbGlyphPosition {.bycopy.} = object
  xAdvance*: int32
  yAdvance*: int32
  xOffset*: int32
  yOffset*: int32
  var1*: uint32
HbMemoryMode {.size: 4.} = enum
  HB_MEMORY_MODE_DUPLICATE = 0, HB_MEMORY_MODE_READONLY = 1,
  HB_MEMORY_MODE_WRITABLE = 2, HB_MEMORY_MODE_READONLY_MAY_MAKE_WRITABLE = 3
HbScript = distinct uint32
ShapedGlyph = object
  glyphIndex*: uint32
  cluster*: uint32
  xAdvance*: float32
  yAdvance*: float32
  xOffset*: float32
  yOffset*: float32
A single glyph after shaping. index is the glyph index in the font's glyph table (NOT a codepoint). cluster is the byte offset in the source UTF-8 string. Advances are in 26.6 fixed-point (1/64 of a pixel) coming out of HarfBuzz; the wrapper converts to pixels via the font's current scale.

Procs

proc hb_blob_create(data: cstring; length: cuint; mode: HbMemoryMode;
                    userData: pointer; destroy: pointer): HbBlob {.importc,
    dynlib: libHarfbuzz, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hb_blob_destroy(blob: HbBlob) {.importc, dynlib: libHarfbuzz, ...raises: [],
                                     tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hb_buffer_add_utf8(buf: HbBuffer; text: cstring; textLen: cint;
                        itemOffset: cuint; itemLen: cint) {.importc,
    dynlib: libHarfbuzz, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hb_buffer_clear_contents(buf: HbBuffer) {.importc, dynlib: libHarfbuzz,
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hb_buffer_create(): HbBuffer {.importc, dynlib: libHarfbuzz, ...raises: [],
                                    tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hb_buffer_destroy(buf: HbBuffer) {.importc, dynlib: libHarfbuzz,
                                        ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hb_buffer_get_glyph_infos(buf: HbBuffer; length: ptr cuint): ptr HbGlyphInfo {.
    importc, dynlib: libHarfbuzz, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hb_buffer_get_glyph_positions(buf: HbBuffer; length: ptr cuint): ptr HbGlyphPosition {.
    importc, dynlib: libHarfbuzz, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hb_buffer_get_length(buf: HbBuffer): cuint {.importc, dynlib: libHarfbuzz,
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hb_buffer_guess_segment_properties(buf: HbBuffer) {.importc,
    dynlib: libHarfbuzz, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hb_buffer_set_direction(buf: HbBuffer; dir: HbDirection) {.importc,
    dynlib: libHarfbuzz, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hb_buffer_set_language(buf: HbBuffer; lang: pointer) {.importc,
    dynlib: libHarfbuzz, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hb_buffer_set_script(buf: HbBuffer; script: HbScript) {.importc,
    dynlib: libHarfbuzz, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hb_face_create(blob: HbBlob; index: cuint): HbFace {.importc,
    dynlib: libHarfbuzz, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hb_face_destroy(face: HbFace) {.importc, dynlib: libHarfbuzz, ...raises: [],
                                     tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hb_font_create(face: HbFace): HbFont {.importc, dynlib: libHarfbuzz,
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hb_font_destroy(font: HbFont) {.importc, dynlib: libHarfbuzz, ...raises: [],
                                     tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hb_font_get_glyph_extents(font: HbFont; glyph: uint32; extents: pointer): cint {.
    importc, dynlib: libHarfbuzz, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hb_font_set_ppem(font: HbFont; xPpem, yPpem: cuint) {.importc,
    dynlib: libHarfbuzz, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hb_font_set_scale(font: HbFont; xScale, yScale: cint) {.importc,
    dynlib: libHarfbuzz, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hb_shape(font: HbFont; buf: HbBuffer; features: pointer; numFeatures: cuint) {.
    importc, dynlib: libHarfbuzz, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc isHarfBuzzAvailable(): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns true if libharfbuzz could be loaded. Caches the result. Safe to call frequently. When false, all shape calls in this module are no-ops and callers fall back to whatever non-shaped path they have (e.g. Pixie typeset). 
proc loadFontFromFile(path: string): HbFont {....raises: [], tags: [ReadIOEffect],
    forbids: [].}
Reads a font file from disk and produces a HarfBuzz font. The face and blob are kept alive by HarfBuzz's internal reference counting (we drop our references but the font retains them). Returns a default-initialized HbFont (with a nil pointer) on failure. 
proc shapeUtf8(font: HbFont; text: string; fontSize: float32): seq[ShapedGlyph] {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Shapes text with font at fontSize pixels. Returns the sequence of glyphs after shaping, with positions in pixels.

The caller owns the font; this proc does not free it. The script and direction are auto-detected via hb_buffer_guess_segment_properties.

Returns an empty seq if HarfBuzz is not available.


Made with Nim. Generated: 2026-06-09 05:16:10 UTC
